citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
A male and female pygmy hippopotamus, Motto and Gloria, have several open and bleeding wounds on the lower rear
legs. The area around the wounds appears red and swollen. Open wounds can become contaminated leading to infection
and disease. The facility had not observed the lesions prior to the inspection.
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation
of all animals to assess their health and well-being. A mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that
timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending
veterinarian.
The two pygmy hippopotamuses must be evaluated by a licensed veterinarian by February 14, 2026, and treated in an
appropriate manner depending on the recommendation of the veterinarian.

At the time of inspection, a male and female pygmy hippopotamus, Motto and Gloria, were housed in an indoor enclosure
measuring approximately 8ft x 10 ft. The enclosure had an excessive accumulation of feces and urine with a strong smell
of ammonia. The animals could not avoid being in contact with waste material due to the large amount covering the floor
area. The two animals had waste matter smeared over a large portion of their bodies.
Excreta shall be removed from primary enclosures as often as necessary to prevent contamination of the animals
contained therein and to minimize disease hazards and to reduce odors.
Corrected at time of inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
A male and female pygmy hippopotamus, Motto and Gloria, have several open and bleeding wounds on the lower rear 
legs. The area around the wounds appears red and swollen. Open wounds can become contaminated leading to infection 
and disease. The facility had not observed the lesions prior to the inspection. 
 
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation 
of all animals to assess their health and well-being. A mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that 
timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending 
veterinarian. 
 
The two pygmy hippopotamuses must be evaluated by a licensed veterinarian by February 14, 2026, and treated in an 
appropriate manner depending on the recommendation of the veterinarian. 
3.131(a)  Direct
   
Sanitation. 
At the time of inspection, a male and female pygmy hippopotamus, Motto and Gloria, were housed in an indoor enclosure 
measuring approximately 8ft x 10 ft. The enclosure had an excessive accumulation of feces and urine with a strong smell 
of ammonia. The animals could not avoid being in contact with waste material due to the large amount covering the floor 
area. The two animals had waste matter smeared over a large portion of their bodies. 
 
Excreta shall be removed from primary enclosures as often as necessary to prevent contamination of the animals 
contained therein and to minimize disease hazards and to reduce odors. 
 
Corrected at time of inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.

n
